caption: The video displays a static educational slide for a course on "CRYOGENIC ENGINEERING," specifically "Tutorial – 2." The slide focuses on calculating the "Work/unit mass of gas liquefied," featuring a temperature-entropy (T-S) diagram. The diagram shows a cycle with paths labeled "T=const," "D=const," "p=const," and "h=const," with numbered points from 1 to 4. To the left of the diagram, given values are listed: "W/m = 463" and "y = 0.074." Below these, a mathematical equation is shown: "-W/m_L = W/y*m = 463/0.074 = 6265.22 J/g." The NPTEL logo is visible in the bottom left corner, and the footer identifies "Prof. M D Atrey, Department of Mechanical Engineering, IIT Bombay." There are no people or physical objects present, and the scene remains completely unchanged across all frames, indicating a still image being displayed during a presentation.
